channel.title: Speak Malayalam: Phrase of the Day
channel.description: Bite-size real colloquial Malayalam — one everyday phrase or dialogue per episode, spoken clearly. By Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD (Applied Linguistics), teacher of 3,000+ learners worldwide. Full guides at speakmalayalam.com.
channel.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair
channel.itunes:summary: Bite-size colloquial Malayalam phrases with native pronunciation.

item.title: Namaskaram — the perfect Malayalam hello
item.description: Learn the warm, all-purpose Malayalam greeting used across Kerala. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Sukhamaano? — how are you?
item.description: The friendly check-in you will hear in every Malayali conversation. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Nandi & Valare Nandi — thank you
item.description: Say thank you warmly in Malayalam. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Njaan ninne snehikkunnu — I love you
item.description: The most beautiful sentence in Malayalam, pronounced right. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Enikku ninne ishtamaanu — I like you
item.description: The everyday way Malayalis actually express affection. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Amma & Achan — mother and father
item.description: The two words every Malayali heart is built on. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Mole, choru kazhicho? — have you eaten?
item.description: The classic Malayali family love-question. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Ithu enthaa? — what is this?
item.description: Your all-purpose curiosity tool in Malayalam. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Nee evideyaa? — where are you?
item.description: The opening line of every Malayali phone call. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Adipoli! — awesome!
item.description: The slang that makes Malayalis grin when learners use it. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Machane! — bro!
item.description: Friendly slang between friends, used everywhere. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Chetta, oru chaaya — ordering tea
item.description: Order tea like a local at any Kerala tea shop. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Vishakkunnu! — I’m hungry!
item.description: The dinner-table essential. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Nalla ruchi! — so tasty!
item.description: The compliment every Malayali cook wants to hear. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Evideya bus stand? — asking directions
item.description: Travel Kerala with confidence. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Vila kurayumo? — bargaining
item.description: The shopping phrase that saves you money. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Counting 1–5 in Malayalam
item.description: Onnu, randu, moonu, naalu, anchu — counting made musical. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: Pathukke parayu — speak slowly
item.description: The learner’s best friend phrase. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: The Amma phone call — full dialogue
item.description: A complete, real Malayali family phone conversation. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair

item.title: At the tea shop — full dialogue
item.description: A complete tea-shop exchange, start to finish. From speakmalayalam.com — colloquial Malayalam with Dr. Reshmi R Nair, PhD.
item.itunes:author: Dr. Reshmi R Nair
